# Tokio Functional Parity Contracts

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.2.1` ([T1.2.a])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Dependencies**: T1.1.a (inventory), T1.1.b (evidence), T1.1.c (risk)
**Purpose**: Domain-specific functional contracts describing required behavior
for Tokio-ecosystem parity. Each contract is precise enough to convert directly
into conformance tests.

---

## 1. Contract Format

Each capability domain receives a contract with:

- **MUST** requirements: mandatory for parity claim
- **SHOULD** requirements: important for production credibility
- **MAY** requirements: nice-to-have, not blocking
- **Asupersync-specific**: behaviors that differ from Tokio by design

Contracts use verifiable language: "the implementation MUST return X when Y"
rather than "should generally handle Z."

---

## 2. Domain Contracts

### C01 — Core Runtime

**Scope**: Task spawning, work-stealing scheduler, blocking pool, shutdown.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C01.1 | MUST | `spawn()` returns a `TaskHandle` that can be awaited or cancelled |
| C01.2 | MUST | Work-stealing scheduler distributes tasks across worker threads |
| C01.3 | MUST | `spawn_blocking()` offloads closure to dedicated thread pool; does not block async workers |
| C01.4 | MUST | Runtime shutdown drains all pending tasks with bounded cleanup budget |
| C01.5 | MUST | Panics in spawned tasks are captured as `Outcome::Panicked`, not propagated to runtime |
| C01.6 | SHOULD | Thread count is configurable via builder and environment variables |
| C01.7 | SHOULD | Worker thread parking/unparking is adaptive (no busy-wait or excessive sleep) |
| C01.8 | Asupersync | All spawned tasks are region-owned; orphan tasks are structurally impossible |
| C01.9 | Asupersync | `Cx` capability context is required for spawn; no ambient `Handle::current()` |

### C02 — Structured Concurrency and Cancellation

**Scope**: Regions, scopes, cancellation protocol, obligations.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C02.1 | MUST | Region close blocks until all owned tasks reach terminal state (quiescence) |
| C02.2 | MUST | Cancellation is a three-phase protocol: request → drain → finalize |
| C02.3 | MUST | Cancel requests are idempotent; repeated requests can only strengthen (never weaken) severity |
| C02.4 | MUST | Parent cancellation propagates to all child regions and tasks |
| C02.5 | MUST | Race losers receive `CancelKind::RaceLost` and drain correctly |
| C02.6 | MUST | Obligation leak check detects unfulfilled obligations at region close |
| C02.7 | MUST | `checkpoint()` returns `Err(Cancelled)` when cancel is requested and mask is zero |
| C02.8 | MUST | `mask()` defers cancellation; checkpoint succeeds while masked |
| C02.9 | Asupersync | No Tokio equivalent exists; these are Asupersync invariants, not parity requirements |

### C03 — Channels

**Scope**: mpsc, oneshot, broadcast, watch.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C03.1 | MUST | Bounded mpsc: `send()` blocks when buffer is full; `recv()` blocks when empty |
| C03.2 | MUST | Unbounded mpsc: `send()` never blocks; `recv()` blocks when empty |
| C03.3 | MUST | Oneshot: single value delivery; sender drop causes `RecvError::Closed` |
| C03.4 | MUST | Broadcast: all active receivers see every message sent after subscription |
| C03.5 | MUST | Watch: receivers see the most recent value; `changed()` waits for updates |
| C03.6 | MUST | Sender drop closes the channel; receivers observe closure |
| C03.7 | MUST | All receivers drop → sender `send()` returns error |
| C03.8 | SHOULD | `try_send()` and `try_recv()` are non-blocking variants |
| C03.9 | Asupersync | Two-phase `reserve()`/`send()` for cancel-safe channel operations |

### C04 — Synchronization Primitives

**Scope**: Mutex, RwLock, Semaphore, Notify, Barrier, OnceCell.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C04.1 | MUST | `Mutex::lock()` provides exclusive access; drop releases lock |
| C04.2 | MUST | `RwLock`: multiple concurrent readers OR one exclusive writer |
| C04.3 | MUST | `Semaphore::acquire(n)` blocks until `n` permits available |
| C04.4 | MUST | `Notify::notify_one()` wakes exactly one waiter (or stores permit if none waiting) |
| C04.5 | MUST | `Notify::notify_waiters()` wakes all current waiters |
| C04.6 | MUST | `Barrier::wait()` blocks until N parties arrive |
| C04.7 | MUST | `OnceCell::get_or_init()` executes initializer exactly once, even under contention |
| C04.8 | SHOULD | `try_lock()` and `try_read()` are non-blocking variants |
| C04.9 | Asupersync | All wait operations are cancel-aware via `Cx` |

### C05 — Time and Timers

**Scope**: sleep, timeout, interval, instant.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C05.1 | MUST | `sleep(duration)` completes after at least `duration` has elapsed |
| C05.2 | MUST | `timeout(duration, future)` returns `Err` if future does not complete within duration |
| C05.3 | MUST | `interval(period)` ticks at regular intervals; missed ticks follow configured policy |
| C05.4 | MUST | Timer resolution matches configured wheel granularity |
| C05.5 | SHOULD | Interval supports burst, delay, and skip missed-tick policies |
| C05.6 | Asupersync | LabRuntime uses virtual time: `sleep(1s)` advances virtual clock, completes instantly |

### C06 — Async I/O

**Scope**: AsyncRead, AsyncWrite, utility operators.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C06.1 | MUST | `AsyncRead::poll_read()` fills buffer from underlying source |
| C06.2 | MUST | `AsyncWrite::poll_write()` writes buffer to underlying sink |
| C06.3 | MUST | `AsyncWrite::poll_flush()` ensures all buffered data is written |
| C06.4 | MUST | `AsyncWrite::poll_shutdown()` performs graceful stream close |
| C06.5 | MUST | `copy()` transfers bytes from reader to writer until EOF |
| C06.6 | MUST | `split()` divides a read+write stream into independent halves |
| C06.7 | SHOULD | `BufReader`/`BufWriter` reduce syscall count via buffering |
| C06.8 | SHOULD | `lines()` yields newline-delimited strings |
| C06.9 | Asupersync | `write_permit` provides two-phase write for cancel safety |

### C07 — Codec and Framing

**Scope**: Decoder, Encoder, Framed.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C07.1 | MUST | `Decoder::decode()` extracts frames from a byte buffer |
| C07.2 | MUST | `Encoder::encode()` serializes items into byte buffer |
| C07.3 | MUST | `Framed` wraps an I/O stream with encoder+decoder pair |
| C07.4 | SHOULD | LinesCodec, BytesCodec, LengthDelimitedCodec are available |
| C07.5 | SHOULD | Codec back-pressure propagates correctly through framed streams |

### C08 — Byte Buffers

**Scope**: Bytes, BytesMut, Buf, BufMut.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C08.1 | MUST | `Bytes` is a cheaply cloneable, reference-counted byte slice |
| C08.2 | MUST | `BytesMut` allows mutable access to owned byte buffer |
| C08.3 | MUST | `Buf` trait: `remaining()`, `chunk()`, `advance()` |
| C08.4 | MUST | `BufMut` trait: `remaining_mut()`, `chunk_mut()`, `put_slice()` |
| C08.5 | MUST | `BytesMut::freeze()` converts to immutable `Bytes` |

### C09 — Reactor

**Scope**: I/O event notification, platform backends.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C09.1 | MUST | Reactor translates OS I/O readiness events into task wakeups |
| C09.2 | MUST | Registration persists across poll cycles until explicit deregister |
| C09.3 | MUST | Stale wakeups (deregistered fd) are silently discarded |
| C09.4 | SHOULD | epoll (Linux), kqueue (macOS/BSD), IOCP (Windows) backends |
| C09.5 | SHOULD | io_uring support for Linux 5.1+ (optional) |
| C09.6 | Asupersync | Lab reactor provides fully deterministic I/O event simulation |

### C10 — TCP / UDP / Unix

**Scope**: Socket types, connection lifecycle.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C10.1 | MUST | `TcpListener::bind()` + `accept()` yields connected `TcpStream` |
| C10.2 | MUST | `TcpStream::connect()` establishes TCP connection |
| C10.3 | MUST | `UdpSocket::bind()` + `send_to()`/`recv_from()` for datagrams |
| C10.4 | MUST | `UnixListener`/`UnixStream` for local IPC (Unix platforms) |
| C10.5 | MUST | Socket options configurable (nodelay, reuseaddr, buffer sizes) |
| C10.6 | SHOULD | `TcpStream::split()` for concurrent read+write |
| C10.7 | SHOULD | Graceful shutdown via `shutdown(Write)` |

### C11 — DNS

**Scope**: Name resolution.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C11.1 | MUST | Resolve hostname to IP addresses (A/AAAA records) |
| C11.2 | SHOULD | DNS result caching with TTL |
| C11.3 | SHOULD | Happy Eyeballs (RFC 8305) for dual-stack connection racing |

### C12 — TLS

**Scope**: TLS handshake, certificate verification, ALPN.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C12.1 | MUST | `TlsConnector::connect()` performs TLS handshake over async stream |
| C12.2 | MUST | `TlsAcceptor::accept()` performs server-side TLS handshake |
| C12.3 | MUST | Server name verification against certificate |
| C12.4 | MUST | ALPN negotiation for protocol selection |
| C12.5 | MUST | Resulting `TlsStream` implements `AsyncRead + AsyncWrite` |
| C12.6 | SHOULD | Certificate pinning support |
| C12.7 | SHOULD | Client certificate authentication |
| C12.8 | SHOULD | Configurable root certificate sources (native, webpki) |

### C13 — WebSocket

**Scope**: WebSocket handshake, framing, close protocol.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C13.1 | MUST | Client: HTTP upgrade handshake per RFC 6455 |
| C13.2 | MUST | Server: accept upgrade and complete handshake |
| C13.3 | MUST | Frame types: Text, Binary, Ping, Pong, Close |
| C13.4 | MUST | Close handshake with status code propagation |
| C13.5 | SHOULD | Per-message compression (permessage-deflate) |
| C13.6 | SHOULD | Message fragmentation/defragmentation |

### C14 — HTTP/1.1 + HTTP/2

**Scope**: Request/response lifecycle, connection management.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C14.1 | MUST | HTTP/1.1: request/response with chunked transfer encoding |
| C14.2 | MUST | HTTP/1.1: keep-alive connection reuse |
| C14.3 | MUST | HTTP/2: multiplexed streams over single connection |
| C14.4 | MUST | HTTP/2: HPACK header compression |
| C14.5 | MUST | HTTP/2: flow control (stream and connection level) |
| C14.6 | MUST | HTTP/2: SETTINGS, PING, GOAWAY frame handling |
| C14.7 | MUST | Connection pooling with health checks |
| C14.8 | SHOULD | Response body streaming |
| C14.9 | SHOULD | Content compression (gzip, deflate, br) |

### C15 — QUIC + HTTP/3

**Scope**: QUIC transport, HTTP/3 framing.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C15.1 | MUST | QUIC handshake with TLS 1.3 |
| C15.2 | MUST | Bidirectional and unidirectional streams |
| C15.3 | MUST | Loss detection and recovery (RFC 9002) |
| C15.4 | MUST | Congestion control |
| C15.5 | MUST | HTTP/3 framing over QUIC streams |
| C15.6 | MUST | QPACK header compression |
| C15.7 | SHOULD | 0-RTT connection resumption |
| C15.8 | SHOULD | Connection migration |
| C15.9 | SHOULD | Key update |

### C16 — Web Framework

**Scope**: Routing, extractors, middleware, server lifecycle.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C16.1 | MUST | Path-based routing with parameter extraction |
| C16.2 | MUST | Method-based routing (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) |
| C16.3 | MUST | Request body extraction (JSON, form, bytes) |
| C16.4 | MUST | Response serialization with status codes and headers |
| C16.5 | MUST | Middleware/layer composition (before/after request processing) |
| C16.6 | MUST | Graceful shutdown with connection draining |
| C16.7 | SHOULD | Path prefix nesting (router composition) |
| C16.8 | SHOULD | Static file serving |
| C16.9 | Asupersync | Request-scoped regions for structured cleanup on disconnect |

### C17 — gRPC

**Scope**: Unary/streaming RPCs, health checks.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C17.1 | MUST | Unary RPC: request → response |
| C17.2 | MUST | Server streaming: request → stream of responses |
| C17.3 | MUST | Client streaming: stream of requests → response |
| C17.4 | MUST | Bidirectional streaming |
| C17.5 | MUST | gRPC status codes and error propagation |
| C17.6 | MUST | gRPC Health Check protocol (grpc.health.v1) |
| C17.7 | SHOULD | Deadline/timeout propagation |
| C17.8 | SHOULD | Request/response compression |
| C17.9 | SHOULD | Interceptors for auth, logging, tracing |

### C18 — Database Clients

**Scope**: Connection, query execution, transactions.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C18.1 | MUST | PostgreSQL: connect, query, parameterized statements |
| C18.2 | MUST | MySQL: connect, query, parameterized statements |
| C18.3 | MUST | SQLite: async wrapper over blocking operations |
| C18.4 | MUST | Transaction begin/commit/rollback |
| C18.5 | MUST | Row-level result iteration |
| C18.6 | SHOULD | Connection pooling with configurable limits |
| C18.7 | SHOULD | Prepared statement caching |
| C18.8 | SHOULD | TLS-encrypted connections (PostgreSQL, MySQL) |

### C19 — Messaging Clients

**Scope**: Pub/sub, message queues.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C19.1 | MUST | Redis: GET, SET, PUB/SUB, Lua scripts |
| C19.2 | MUST | NATS: publish, subscribe, request-reply |
| C19.3 | MUST | Kafka: produce, consume, consumer groups |
| C19.4 | SHOULD | NATS JetStream: durable subscriptions |
| C19.5 | SHOULD | Redis Streams support |
| C19.6 | SHOULD | AMQP 0-9-1 client (RabbitMQ) |
| C19.7 | MAY | MQTT 3.1.1/5.0 client |

### C20 — Service / Middleware

**Scope**: Service trait, layers, adapters.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C20.1 | MUST | Service trait: `call(Request) → Future<Response>` |
| C20.2 | MUST | Layer trait: wraps service with additional behavior |
| C20.3 | MUST | Timeout layer: fails request after duration |
| C20.4 | MUST | Rate limiting: token bucket or sliding window |
| C20.5 | MUST | Concurrency limiting: max in-flight requests |
| C20.6 | MUST | Load shedding: reject when overloaded |
| C20.7 | MUST | Retry: configurable retry policy with backoff |
| C20.8 | SHOULD | Tower compatibility adapter (bidirectional) |
| C20.9 | Asupersync | Service calls pass `Cx` for capability-scoped cancellation |

### C21 — Filesystem

**Scope**: Async file I/O, directory operations.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C21.1 | MUST | `File::open()` and `File::create()` for async file I/O |
| C21.2 | MUST | `read()`, `write()`, `read_to_string()` convenience functions |
| C21.3 | MUST | `read_dir()` for directory listing |
| C21.4 | MUST | `create_dir_all()` and `remove_file()` |
| C21.5 | MUST | `metadata()` for file/directory attributes |
| C21.6 | SHOULD | `OpenOptions` builder for fine-grained file opening |
| C21.7 | SHOULD | `copy()`, `rename()`, `symlink()`, `canonicalize()` |
| C21.8 | Asupersync | VFS layer for deterministic filesystem testing in LabRuntime |

### C22 — Process Management

**Scope**: Spawning and managing child processes.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C22.1 | MUST | `Command::new().spawn()` launches child process |
| C22.2 | MUST | `Child::wait()` awaits process completion |
| C22.3 | MUST | `Child::kill()` terminates process |
| C22.4 | MUST | Stdin/stdout/stderr piping |
| C22.5 | SHOULD | `output()` convenience for capture-and-wait |
| C22.6 | SHOULD | Exit status code inspection |

### C23 — Signals

**Scope**: OS signal handling.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C23.1 | MUST | `ctrl_c()` future resolves on SIGINT |
| C23.2 | MUST | Unix signal handling for SIGTERM, SIGHUP, SIGUSR1/2 |
| C23.3 | SHOULD | Graceful shutdown coordination via signal-triggered workflow |

### C24 — Streams

**Scope**: Async iterators and adapters.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C24.1 | MUST | `Stream` trait: `poll_next()` yields `Option<Item>` |
| C24.2 | MUST | `map()`, `filter()`, `filter_map()` adapters |
| C24.3 | MUST | `merge()` for combining multiple streams |
| C24.4 | MUST | `chunks()` for batching |
| C24.5 | MUST | `take()`, `skip()` for windowing |
| C24.6 | MUST | `collect()` into containers |
| C24.7 | SHOULD | `buffered()` and `buffer_unordered()` for concurrent processing |
| C24.8 | SHOULD | `zip()`, `chain()`, `enumerate()`, `fuse()` |

### C25 — Observability

**Scope**: Metrics, tracing, diagnostics.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C25.1 | MUST | Native metrics collection (counters, gauges, histograms) |
| C25.2 | MUST | Tracing compatibility for structured logging |
| C25.3 | SHOULD | OpenTelemetry metrics export |
| C25.4 | SHOULD | Runtime task/obligation inspection API |
| C25.5 | Asupersync | Spectral health monitoring for wait-graph anomaly detection |

### C26 — Lab / Deterministic Testing

**Scope**: Deterministic runtime, virtual time, oracles.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C26.1 | MUST | LabRuntime executes async code deterministically given seed |
| C26.2 | MUST | Virtual time: `sleep()` advances clock without wall-clock wait |
| C26.3 | MUST | Same seed + same code = identical execution trace |
| C26.4 | MUST | Oracle suite: obligation leak, quiescence, cancel protocol checks |
| C26.5 | SHOULD | Chaos injection: random delays, failures, reordering |
| C26.6 | SHOULD | Trace replay from recorded execution |
| C26.7 | Asupersync | No Tokio equivalent; this is unique infrastructure |

### C27 — Resilience Combinators

**Scope**: Circuit breaker, bulkhead, retry, hedge, etc.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C27.1 | MUST | Circuit breaker: closed → open → half-open state machine |
| C27.2 | MUST | Bulkhead: concurrency isolation per partition |
| C27.3 | MUST | Retry with configurable policy (count, backoff, jitter) |
| C27.4 | MUST | Timeout combinator: deadline-bounded execution |
| C27.5 | MUST | Race: concurrent execution, first success wins |
| C27.6 | MUST | Join: concurrent execution, all must succeed |
| C27.7 | SHOULD | Hedge: speculative retry for latency reduction |
| C27.8 | SHOULD | Quorum: N-of-M success required |
| C27.9 | Asupersync | Algebraic laws (associativity, commutativity) verified by property tests |

### C28 — Tokio Interop

**Scope**: Compatibility with Tokio-locked crates.

| # | Req | Contract |
|---|-----|----------|
| C28.1 | MUST | Provide Tokio-compatible `Handle` shim for `tokio::spawn` |
| C28.2 | MUST | Provide Tokio-compatible I/O traits (`tokio::io::AsyncRead/Write`) |
| C28.3 | MUST | Provide Tokio-compatible timer (`tokio::time`) shim |
| C28.4 | MUST | Tower service adapter (bidirectional) |
| C28.5 | SHOULD | `reqwest`-compatible HTTP client surface |
| C28.6 | SHOULD | `sqlx` runtime adapter |
| C28.7 | SHOULD | Documented list of tested third-party crate compatibility |

## 3. Contract Verification Mapping

Each MUST requirement maps to a conformance test category:

| Contract Prefix | Test Suite Location | Status |
|----------------|---------------------|--------|
| C01 | `conformance/src/tests/runtime.rs` + `tests/runtime_e2e.rs` | Partial |
| C02 | `conformance/src/tests/cancellation.rs` + `tests/cancel_*.rs` | Good |
| C03 | `conformance/src/tests/channels.rs` + `tests/channel_*.rs` | Good |
| C04 | `tests/sync_*.rs` + inline | Good |
| C05 | `tests/time_e2e.rs` + inline | Good |
| C06 | `conformance/src/tests/io.rs` + `tests/io_*.rs` | Partial |
| C07 | `tests/codec_e2e.rs` | Needs expansion |
| C08 | Inline only | Needs integration tests |
| C09 | `tests/io_uring_reactor.rs` + inline | Partial |
| C10 | `tests/net_*.rs` | Good |
| C11 | Inline only | Needs expansion |
| C12 | `tests/tls_conformance.rs` | Good |
| C13 | `tests/e2e_websocket.rs` + `tests/e2e/websocket/` | Good |
| C14 | `tests/http_*.rs` | Good |
| C15 | `tests/quic_h3_*.rs` | Partial (parked) |
| C16 | `tests/e2e_web.rs` | Needs expansion |
| C17 | `tests/grpc_*.rs` | Good |
| C18 | `tests/e2e_database*.rs` | Partial |
| C19 | `tests/e2e_messaging.rs` | Needs expansion |
| C20 | `tests/service_verification.rs` | Good |
| C21 | `tests/e2e_fs.rs` + `tests/fs_verification.rs` | Partial |
| C22 | `tests/compile_test_process.rs` | Needs expansion |
| C23 | `tests/e2e_signal.rs` | Partial |
| C24 | `tests/stream_e2e.rs` | Good |
| C25 | Inline only | Needs integration tests |
| C26 | `tests/lab_*.rs` | Good |
| C27 | `tests/e2e_combinator.rs` + `tests/algebraic_laws.rs` | Good |
| C28 | N/A | Not yet implemented |
